name: flow/flow
description: The declarative, AI-first flow surface — render a node/edge graph from plain serializable data.

meta.name: Flow
meta.description: The declarative, AI-first flow surface — render a node/edge graph from plain serializable data.

props.name: onNodesChange
props.description: Called with the next nodes when they change (drag, add, remove).

props.name: onEdgesChange
props.description: Called with the next edges when they change.

props.name: onConnect
props.description: Called when two handles are connected to form a new edge.

props.name: nodeTypes
props.description: Custom node renderers keyed by node.type.

props.name: fitView
props.description: When true, fits the graph to the viewport on mount.

props.name: background
props.description: Background pattern — true for the default dots, or a config object.

props.name: minimap
props.description: When true, shows the minimap overlay.

props.name: interactive
props.description: When false (view mode), nodes cannot be selected, dragged, or connected and handles are hidden; pan/zoom still work.

props.name: className
props.description: Additional CSS class names merged onto the root element.

examples.title: Declarative pipeline
examples.description: A flow from plain serializable data, with background, controls, and an animated edge.

examples.title: Layered layout
examples.description: Let the dependency-free layered layout arrange a small DAG.

whenToUse: Rendering a graph from data — the common case and the agent-emittable surface
Flowcharts, DAGs, pipelines, and mind-maps from plain nodes/edges arrays
whenNotToUse: A scripted, sequenced walkthrough — use FlowStory
Heavy graph editing (undo/redo, resize/rotate) — out of scope

antiPatterns.bad: Imperatively building the graph with graph.addNode(...)
antiPatterns.why: Declarative serializable data is what an agent can emit and what stays in sync with props.

intent.a11yRationale: role="application" canvas; nodes are focusable groups; controls are real i18n-labeled buttons.
